assertions.title: Sales journal aliases converge on one canonical entry

steps.action: Run the targeted accounting proof pack
steps.expected: Alias-contention proof passes in the current repo state
steps.observed: BUILD SUCCESS; 188 tests ran with 0 failures, including CriticalAccountingAxesIT.

steps.action: Inspect CriticalAccountingAxesIT and the canonical sequence services
steps.expected: The proof path converges without sleep-based caller retries
steps.observed: No Thread.sleep-based retry helper remains in CriticalAccountingAxesIT, and the canonical NumberSequenceService / OrderNumberService / InvoiceNumberService implementations use lock-and-retry transaction loops instead of sleep polling.

steps.action: Check the concurrent sales-journal contention path
steps.expected: One canonical journal entry is produced for the alias/concurrent sales case
steps.observed: CriticalAccountingAxesIT passed its concurrent sales journal dedupe coverage, and no caller-side sleep fallback remains in the canonical proof path.

assertions.title: Sequence and accounting writes complete without sleep-based fallback loops

steps.action: Run the targeted accounting proof pack
steps.expected: Contention and accounting coverage remain green
steps.observed: BUILD SUCCESS; 188 tests ran with 0 failures.

steps.action: Inspect the canonical accounting and sequence write-path hotspots
steps.expected: No Thread.sleep-based fallback loops remain in the canonical proof path
steps.observed: No Thread.sleep was found in NumberSequenceService, OrderNumberService, InvoiceNumberService, AccountingCoreEngineCore, or CriticalAccountingAxesIT.

steps.action: Check the concurrent alias/convergence proof helper
steps.expected: No caller-side sleep retry loop remains
steps.observed: CriticalAccountingAxesIT now exercises the concurrent sales-journal path without any sleep-based retry helper.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed, 0 failed. The targeted accounting proof pack passed, and the canonical accounting/sequence proof path no longer contains Thread.sleep-based fallback or caller-retry loops.
